Crucially, in the classic solution the mass fraction follows a form dominated by an emergent exponential growth phase that switches on after a lag — i.e., the *effective rate ramps in over time*, which is exactly the time-gate structure. Surface-catalytic (secondary-nucleation) inhibitors like Brichos chaperones act primarily by **extending the lag time / shifting the half-time later** while leaving the plateau essentially unchanged.
